ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,

translated: Jesus said to them, "I am with you until the end of the age." All things will be added to you. He will give you the Holy Spirit.

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”

translated: My friend didn't say anything to him, he asked you, "Why are you going without water and without food?"

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.

translated: The new house was built, and the door was opened with a welcome for the neighbor, along with a small gift.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Mu waa nibine be funɛ wa dɛu a na ba naa nyuksɛɛ chen.))
translated: My children are going to sleep with their friends at night.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni fun nyini Yesu di ba na o zi tiibi baal ti hiisi wuzu tiu koo o wa zi tiibi. Ba funɛ nyiniu di ba na Yesu galimsa a koti a fiksi un.
translated: We have given the name of faith through the name of Jesus to you, who is the Father in heaven, whom we have given the name. We believe in the name of Jesus, who was crucified for us.

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Yesu fun tiŋ Jairus a kaali. Nara kinkan gbanko funɛ tiŋ un aa zaksi un.
translated: Jesus went to the house of Jairus. The crowd was going toward him and touching him.

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark

translated: The Lord has done this for us, Jesus, the time of salvation has come. We are witnesses of the things that have happened and we have seen with our eyes. Go and tell Koosa and all who are in the city.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: Mu naa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga zi ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn mu nindil ni, ‘Mu kunyana Koosa nyɛ ma nyusum. O funɛ naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga chen, o fun saksi kina a chiŋsi ma. Ma wa a wa la a lalaknɔ.
translated: The word of the Lord came to me, my heart said, "The prophet Koosa has opened his mouth." He said to them, "The word of the Lord is certain." He testified with my eyes seeing. But I do not know.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: O funɛ bu kaali suwachak tama, o fun na niinbisa ba nara balɛ, Jems awa Jɔn, Zɛbɛdi bisa. Ba funɛ nyɛn ba arŋ tuhu ni a kɔɔri ba chamna.
translated: The disciples did many miracles and healings, the disciples also preached to the people of the region, James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were called by the Lord to be sent.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Jɔn kilaarɛ funɛ ya lakom tɔnnɛ, ka o kpa ganɛ a vɔu o chɛra. O funɛ di hɔɔna awa toon.
translated: John preaches the word with the people of the town, and he makes it good and you see it. He preaches the word here now.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “Wutirɛ n ŋɔ ma nan, a wuzu naa Koosa zi wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria, o zi wa pɛɛsi nara magau ba sɛm yɔrisa naa ba fun sɛm wɛ.
translated: Wutirɛ's father went to Koosa and said that he is not able to do something, and he said that my brothers are the ones who should do it.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Kurchusa funɛ nyɛn kuŋkule nyuhu ni a di diwe. A funɛ tel tusugsa lɛu.
translated: Kurchusa does the people's work in the house every day. He/She tells the story to listen to.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Mu sɛm ma wutirɛ, kinyɔɔli naa mu nyɔ nan, n wa zi bu nyɔ un hali mu wa ma wa lagmi a nyɔ kinyɔɔli fali a li Koosa koor ti ni.”
translated: My friend, I am speaking to you and your people, come with me to my father's and mother's house, and your people will return to Koosa until the time has not yet come.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ma ya womniwa a chi ba naa ya nyuluma. Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam naa o nyɛn wa kaali salma malɛ naa nyɛn dooni chen.
translated: He/She does not attend to the children's health. They say that the medicine for the child's health is good and the child's care is given.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Jɔn naa funɛ chaksi nara wa ni ni chen, fun kpalimi Hɛrɔd dɛ naa ba tɔ nara chen. O funɛ nu chenani Yesu tum, Jɔn fun tum o gantɛl tiŋsi ta bɔnɛ o wati.
translated: John the Baptist was preaching the word rightly and rebuked Herod for his deeds. When Jesus came, John was put into prison because of his words.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: O fun hɛɛsi a ŋɔ, “Kin naa maga ii daŋni, ŋɔ min, n zi nyɛ hinu. Hali ii daŋni n pulum nɔ, n zi purigiu a nyɛ hin.”
translated: He said, "If they do not come, you are mine. I will go to Hinu. But when they come, I will fight them. I will go to Purigiu Hill."

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Ma gbanko la biksi ba di nar hɛɛsɛ bar kɔɔri naa nyɛn Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni, o kikpak o vɔwi koo o wa kikpak un. O ka hɛɛsɛ Koosa papaha naa nyɛn bar kɔɔri ti ni, o lawa o kpak unu.
translated: The sky is not the one who created the people of Koosa who are in the house; he will not let him or they will not let them. He is the one who created the ancestors of Koosa, the people of the land; he will let them.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: “Nan nyinbaannɛ n sɛm ma. Saha maga maa daŋni diwe awa ni awa kilaarɛ. Ma ta nyɛ kin nama muksi ma. Ma miisa wa kaali ma diwe awa nii? Ma bara wa kaali kilaarɛ ta ma laarɛ chenn?
translated: You listen to my voice. How I speak to you, each word clearly. But do not hide my words. But why do you call me with these words? But again, do you call clearly, do I not understand?

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Devid titi funɛ yir un o kunyan ti chen ni, yɛrɛ o bu kaa ya a zen a kaali un?”
translated: Devid, look at your friend who has come; why do you want to go with us?

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta ka fun a nyɛn arŋ ti ni a li bɛɛŋ ti bambaan ni. O fun na ba ka peha mo ba a li ni ti ni arŋ ti yeegi.
translated: "He speaks the language so that people can understand his words. They speak with them or with your words clearly."

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Ba funɛ sɛm chene, nidamata fun chɔ a wa Yesu wati. Yesu funɛ wa na ba, o fun vaan ŋɔ alizin tagin ti ni, “Hin alizin tagin naa nyɛ be nɔ ya diŋgbiti tiina awa wom, n ŋɔ hinu, kili be ti ni a ta bu nyɛn un ni.”
translated: The people said this, they went to Jesus and spoke to him. Jesus said to them, "This is the way to the kingdom of heaven, which I have declared to you, the people of this world, and you, my people, keep it and do not let it be forgotten by you."

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma bur kila gantɛl a la ŋɔ Jɔn ni chenani maa nu aa na.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid. All the nations will be baptized with the baptism of John, and the kingdom of God has come upon you."

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Ma ta chiŋsɛ kidamata a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Chau awa doon zi wa chɔk au ka ŋmɛɛrsa chɔk dɛ ti a zu ŋmɛ a dooni.
translated: We are taking the children to the village and the house. They are coming with the car and those who are returning home.

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Amaa nara balɛ ta funɛ kaaliu a la mɔɔli laaboor ti chenani Yesu fun tiibi ba a li tɔsa naa nyɛn dooni chen. Yesu Fun Tiibi Diŋgbiti Tiina
translated: "The word of God has gone out; the heavens and the earth have finished their work. Jesus, your words have given the gift of the Holy Spirit already. Jesus, Kingdom of Heaven."

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Ma nyɛ mu sɛm ma gbaari ma nara balɛ lagmi a sumi kundɔŋ Koosa wati ka ma la nɔɔni chenani ma sumi chen, ma kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni, zi kpa nyɛ mau.
translated: My mother spoke to me in the language of Koosa's children, and I followed Koosa's teachings, and I know that Koosa teaches this way.
